---
title: "From Nutella on toast to statistical models"
format:
  html:
    df-print: paged
---

## Introduction

Imagine a piece of toast that’s come out of the toaster with a faint grid pattern pressed in by the rack. We’ll treat each little square in that pattern as a possible outcome in our sample space. Next, we spread 1 Kg of Nutella across the toast so that some squares get a thick coating and others barely any at all.

Wherever the Nutella is **thick**, the probability is **high**; where it’s **thin or absent**, the probability is **low** or **zero**. But taken together, the **total** Nutella spread must sum to 1 Kg.

In this notebook, we’ll model several probability distributions/stastistical models as different ways of smearing Nutella across the toast.

![](images/nutella-toast.webp)

Once we have our sample space and probability distribution, we can:

-   Calculate the probability of any proposition/event in the sample space, i.e., any conclusion given the premises/assumptions/evidence

-   Sample from it to generate data in accordance with the model

## Setup environment

```{r}
#| output: false
library(tidyverse)
library(ggformula)

theme_set(theme_minimal())

set.seed(666)
```

## Helper function

```{r}
plot_model <- function(pdf, n = NULL) {
  n_cells <- nrow(pdf)
  
  # Decide style logic based on total number of cells
  if (n_cells < 100) {
    line_color  <- "black"
    line_width  <- 0.5
    text_size   <- 3
  } else if (n_cells < 200) {
    line_color  <- "black"
    line_width  <- 0.3
    text_size   <- 2
  } else if (n_cells < 500) {
    line_color  <- "black"
    line_width  <- 0.2
    text_size   <- NA
  } else {
    line_color  <- NA
    line_width  <- 0
    text_size   <- NA
  }
  
  # If samples are requested, do not add text labels
  if (!is.null(n)) {
    text_size <- NA
  }
  
  # Determine number of distinct X and Y values for axis text sizing
  n_x <- length(unique(pdf$X))
  n_y <- length(unique(pdf$Y))
  
  axis_text_x_size <- if(n_x < 10) { 12 } else if(n_x < 20) { 10 } else if(n_x < 40) { 8 } else { 6 }
  axis_text_y_size <- if(n_y < 10) { 12 } else if(n_y < 20) { 10 } else if(n_y < 40) { 8 } else { 6 }
  
  max_x <- max(pdf$X)
  max_y <- max(pdf$Y)
  
  # Determine the fill scale parameters based on n_cells:
  # For small grids, the range of p is set higher (midpoint and limit are larger)
  # For larger grids, the scale is "compressed" to better reflect small p values.
  if (n_cells < 100) {
    mid_val   <- 0.25
    limit_val <- 1
  } else if (n_cells < 200) {
    mid_val   <- 0.125
    limit_val <- 0.5
  } else if (n_cells < 500) {
    mid_val   <- 0.0625
    limit_val <- 0.25
  } else {
    mid_val   <- 0.03125
    limit_val <- 0.125
  }
  
  fill_scale <- scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("white", "chocolate4", "black"),
    values = c(0, mid_val, 1),
    limits = c(0, limit_val),
    oob = scales::squish
  )
  
  x_scale <- scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_x))
  y_scale <- scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_y))
  base_theme <- theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = axis_text_x_size, margin = margin(t = 2, b = 0)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = axis_text_y_size, margin = margin(r = 2, l = 0))
  )
  
  # Base tile plot
  plt <- gf_tile(
    Y ~ X,
    data = pdf,
    fill = ~ p,
    color = line_color,
    linewidth = line_width
  ) %>%
    gf_refine(
      fill_scale,
      x_scale,
      y_scale,
      base_theme
    ) +
    expand_limits(x = c(0, max_x), y = c(0, max_y))
  
  # Optionally add text labels if text_size is applicable
  if (!is.na(text_size)) {
    plt <- plt %>% gf_text(label = ~ sprintf("%0.3f", p), size = text_size, alpha = 0.75)
  }
  
  # Optionally overlay random sample points if n is provided
  if (!is.null(n)) {
    plt <- plt %>% gf_point(
      Y ~ X,
      data = pdf %>% slice_sample(n = n, weight_by = p, replace = TRUE),
      size = 4,
      color = "red2",
      alpha = 0.5,
      stroke = 0
    )
  }
  
  return(plt)
}
```

## Simulate data from uniform and linear models

### Grid, model and sampling parameters

```{r}
sample.size <- 50
grid.size <- 10
b0 <- 3
b1 <- 1
s  <- 1
e  <- seq(-s, s) 
sd <- 2
scale <- 1
```

### Create sample space

```{r}
X <- seq(0, grid.size, length.out = grid.size + 1)
Y <- seq(0, grid.size, length.out = grid.size + 1)

ss <-
  expand_grid(X, Y) %>%
  mutate(Ω = paste0("⍵", 1:n())) %>%
  select(Ω, everything())

ss
```

### Uniform model

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = TRUE) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = TRUE) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with no error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = (Y == b0 + b1 * X)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = (Y == b0 + b1 * X)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with fixed error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with fixed error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = abs(Y - (b0 + b1 * X + 0)) <= s) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(M = abs(Y - (b0 + b1 * X + 0)) <= s) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M, p / sum(p[M]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with uniform error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  mutate(p = dunif(Y, min = b0 + b1 * X - s, max = b0 + b1 * X + s)) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% 
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  mutate(p = dunif(Y, min = b0 + b1 * X - s, max = b0 + b1 * X + s)) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% 
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with normal error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  mutate(p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = sd)) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% 
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  mutate(p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = sd)) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% 
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with Cauchy error (heavier tails than the normal)

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  mutate(p = dcauchy(Y, location = b0 + b1 * X, scale = scale)) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% 
  plot_model()
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  mutate(p = dcauchy(Y, location = b0 + b1 * X, scale = scale)) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% 
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

## Print environment

```{r}
sessioninfo::session_info()
```
